details.criteria: Pass: <= 2 years of expenditure. | Warn: 2-5 years. | Fail: > 5 years.
Pass: >= 6 months of operating expenses. | Warn: 3-6 months. | Fail: < 3 months.
Pass: At least one intervention is classified as Tier 1 (Systemic Change) or Tier 2 (Prevention & Scalable Care). | Warn: All verifiable interventions are Tier 3 (Direct Care & Indirect Action).

details.formula: Mapping of verified intervention types to the Intervention Leverage Tier framework.

details.calculation: A quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.criteria: Pass: At least one metric has a quantified counterfactual baseline. | Warn: A qualitative baseline is provided, but lacks quantified data. | Fail: No valid counterfactual baseline is provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline with a source quote.

details.criteria: Pass: Government grants are < 80% of total income. | Fail: Government grants are >= 80% of total income.

details.calculation: Operates in a low-neglectedness / saturated area (Companion Animals: 100%).
details.criteria: Pass: >= 50% of beneficiaries are high-neglectedness (farmed/wild). | Warn: 1-49% are high-neglectedness. | Fail: 0% are high-neglectedness.
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

details.calculation: Organisation self-reported on unintended negative impacts.
details.criteria: Bonus: Organisation self-reported on unintended negative impacts. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found.
details.elaboration: Quote: 'Of course, 2024 was not without its challenges. The ongoing ban on dog imports into Canada, combined with the global cost of living crisis, continued to impact adoption numbers, leading to an 11% decrease compared to the previous year.'
details.formula: Check for self-reported unintended consequences or failures.

details.calculation: No disclosure of euthanasia or live-release rates found.
details.criteria: Bonus: Euthanasia/live-release data is disclosed. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found. | N/A: Not applicable for non-sheltering organisations.
details.elaboration: This check rewards transparency for a sensitive operational metric.
details.formula: For direct rescue orgs, check for euthanasia/live-release rate disclosure.

source.quote: Management and general 62,107
Fundraising 1,279,555
Grants to others $ 8,984,151
Bank and merchant fees 216,102
Program services 9,200,253
Total expenses 10,541,915

Individual and corporate donations $ 8,430,910
Foundation support 15,850
Total revenues and other support 8,541,653
Total assets $ 3,029,071
Total liabilities 2,022,616
Total expenses 10,541,915

Total assets $ 3,029,071
Total liabilities 2,022,616
Net assets, end of year $ 1,006,455
In 2024, our world-leading programme reached a new record—267,100 street dogs and cats received life-saving vaccinations and spay/neuter surgeries.
This year, we treated 22,736 dogs and cats—an increase of 6%, delivering critical medical care, rehabilitation, and comfort to animals in need.
608 new animals became residents following their treatment and recovery
Alongside our trusted partners, Animal Kingdom Foundation, we rescued 24 dogs from the meat trade
successfully rehabilitated 78 dogs, helping them to overcome past trauma, learn to trust, and develop the confidence needed to thrive in homes.
Of the 461 lucky animals who were adopted throughout the year, 302 went to homes within Thailand
23 dogs North America
55 cats North America
77 dogs Europe
2 cats Europe

TO IMPROVE THE WELFARE OF DOGS AND CATS IN ASIA, RESULTING IN BETTER LIVES FOR BOTH THE ANIMAL AND HUMAN COMMUNITIES, TO CREATE A SOCIETY WITHOUT HOMELESS ANIMALS, AND TO ULTIMATELY END ANIMAL CRUELTY.
267,100 dogs and cats neutered
By 2024's end, our numbers are staggering; 1.36 million animals neutered, with over half in the past three years.
By 2024's end, our numbers are staggering; 1.36 million animals neutered, with over half in the past three years.
22,736 dogs and cats treated
The ongoing ban on dog imports into Canada, combined with the global cost of living crisis, continued to impact adoption numbers, leading to an 11% decrease compared to the previous year.
A total of 461 rescued animals - 308 dogs and 153 cats - left the Soi Dog shelter for loving homes in 2024.
43.599 tonnes of food provided for animals affected by the floods
43.599 tonnes of food provided for animals affected by the floods

Of course, 2024 was not without its challenges. The ongoing ban on dog imports into Canada, combined with the global cost of living crisis, continued to impact adoption numbers, leading to an 11% decrease compared to the previous year.
